title: JsonRpcResponse allows result and error both Some, enabling split-brain parsing
description: protocol.rs JsonRpcResponse: both result and error are Option fields with no invariant enforcement. A relay can send {result: ..., error: ...} simultaneously; callers that check resp.error.is_some() before resp.result.is_some() (or vice versa) will misinterpret the response. is_success() correctly requires error.is_none(), but callers that bypass it are silently vulnerable. The struct should enforce mutual exclusivity, e.g. via an enum Result { Ok(Value), Err(JsonRpcError) }.
